title: Upon Sappho Sweetly Playing And Sweetly Singing.
content:
When thou dost play and sweetly sing -
    Whether it be the voice or string
    Or both of them that do agree
    Thus to entrance and ravish me -
    This, this I know, I'm oft struck mute,
    And die away upon thy lute.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Sappho Sweetly Playing And Sweetly Singing."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
